This song actually originated from Mother 3.
author: @zxcyan8137

text: I know, but the original version of the song is just so different from the Brawl version that I just couldn't label it as such  :/
author: @FinnOtaku

text: Um 18 actually came from Mother 3
author: @misterartist1603

text: I know, but the difference between the original version and the one in Smash is just that big  :/
author: @FinnOtaku

text: @FinnOtaku  well, still. It's not really FROM SSBB...
author: @misterartist1603

text: @misterartist1603  Yes it is, that particular remix of that particular song with the same name as the original song is from Super Smash Bros. Brawl. I've also mentioned in the credits that it's a remix
author: @FinnOtaku

text: @FinnOtaku  ohhhh ok.
author: @misterartist1603

text: @FinnOtaku  Sorry about that, I just feel weirded out whenever someone mentions that the music piece originated in another source. We both know this remix came from Brawl but originated from Mother 3, which was what I was used to for the source. Not many people know that Megalovania came from the 2009 Earthbound Halloween Hack by Toby himself tho.
author: @misterartist1603

text:
@misterartist1603  Nah, it's okay, it is a bit misleading after all and as someone who is also really particular about these kind of details, I can see why you'd get a little heated up :D
And I was also aware of that tidbit regarding Megalovania and one of the videos featured in this compilation actually uses that version instead of the Undertale one, but in this case too there are enough differences between these two versions for me to label it as an Undertale song  ^^;
author: @FinnOtaku

text:
@FinnOtaku  heh :p
You know, i remember seeing these old medlies years ago. I was around 12 then! God those were the days...
author: @misterartist1603

text: @misterartist1603  Well I hope you still like these, because I'm gonna release a new one within the next couple of weeks  :D
author: @FinnOtaku

text: @FinnOtaku  Oh heck yes!!! You're doing a good job at these bro! I'm glad awesome people like you exist

text: For me, this is your best medley yet, I can't stop listening to it! I'm curious though.. what songs were left out?
author: @Lolombor1

text: In my sneak peek preview, I had Pushing Onwards and Dans la rue, but those were eventually taken out to make way for songs that I felt to be a bit more relevant. There were also some songs that were included in my WIP playlist but that were taken out, since I thought that the medley would've been too long otherwise, such as Magical Sound Shower, Popcorn, Senbonzakura and 打打打打打打打打打打. For similar reasons, I also had to leave out what I assume to be the opening of No Regrets (巾幗梟雄之義海豪情) and Darkening Dusk, both of which were meant to be included between Bonetrousle and Garten. And during the very end of my arrangement phase, I also tried to add Disco Necropolis, possibly replacing Pettan Fire, but I felt that the BPM would've been too high for that song so I didn't include it.

author: @migj2.022

text: :obama:
author: @FinnOtaku

text: No .FLP File provided? I loved looking at those to gain an understanding on how you built up and structured your song. I would honestly love to see how you did some of the transitions in this, you've improved so much over all this time! I don't know what's up with that like dislike ratio though, how can you dislike this??
author: @ponypuss

text:
I'm still pondering on whether or not to release the FLP, since this time around I used a lot VSTis that aren't built in FL and that aren't freeware, so to some it would sound severely lacking  :/
Also, since you seem to have come in a bit late, you missed the brief time frame when this video got a manual copyright strike for one of the videos featured in this compilation. That issue has now been settled and I'd rather not go over which creator it was, though from what I gathered most of those dislikes came after the video got back up, for whatever reason.
author: @FinnOtaku

text:
Oh sorry to hear that, that sucks.. It may be related to those who claimed it, but I don't see how from their perspective dislikes on a video would be of any benefit.. Anyhow I'd find it really childish if that were the case.. I really do hope the like dislike ratio is gonna balance out a little more, it really deserves more positive feedback, this screams quality in mixing, and sound overall. 

As for the .FLP, not having the plugins would not be that big of a deal, because it may both be a good way to learn about some new VSTs and at the same time, the note data and song structure itself is what I'm really interested in, as everything is so well balanced and mixed. I'm also as mentioned really interested in learning about the transitions, which would rely on note data and automation clips anyway.

Aside of that, I think the note data is the most important if anyone would be downloading/remixing your song given you'd probably not just copy paste the same sounds (I could be wrong, dunno).

Anyway, I can understand if you don't want to release it, it's totally up to you, you've spent a lot of effort on this and you should be able to decide what to do with this in the end.

I just feel kinda bad for the dislikes, hopefully that won't mess up the YouTube algorithm too much... 

I digress, this is art, and your effort really does pay off. 

(Sorry for the longer message)
author: @ponypuss
